summary: MDM4U probability-distribution problems on n independent two-outcome trials use the binomial distribution: P(X=k) = nCk·p^k·(1−p)^(n−k), with mean n·p and standard deviation √(n·p·(1−p)).
method: Check the trials are independent, fixed in number n, with constant success probability p.
Identify n, p and the number of successes k you want.
For a mean or spread, use n·p and √(n·p·(1−p)) directly.
Add the probabilities of several k values for 'at least'/'at most' questions.
